milestones.title: Sanskript can implement bytecode verification in Sanskript

evidence.claim_boundary: Bytecode verification is expressed as a Sanskript corpus example, not yet the production verifier replacing the host verifier.

milestones.title: Sanskript can implement the compiler frontend in Sanskript

evidence.claim_boundary: Compiler frontend corpus is bootstrap evidence, not the production frontend compiled and executed independently of the host compiler.

milestones.title: Sanskript can implement the compiler backend in Sanskript

evidence.claim_boundary: Compiler backend corpus is bootstrap evidence, not the production backend replacing host lowering.

milestones.title: Sanskript can compile its own compiler

evidence.claim_boundary: S1 proves deterministic bootstrap parity for a subset; it is not a Sanskript-authored compiler binary compiling the full compiler.

milestones.title: Sanskript can run its own VM

evidence.claim_boundary: SanskriptSubsetVM evidence is bootstrap/subset execution, not the full Sanskript VM running itself.

milestones.title: Sanskript can build and test itself

evidence.claim_boundary: Bootstrap test-runner example runs under the host CLI; it is not yet a native Sanskript build-and-test loop for the full repository.

milestones.title: Sanskript can emit native binaries for at least one platform

notes: runtime-startup: planned for x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
entrypoint: mainCRTStartup -> _sanskript_main
native-exceptions: structured-exception-plan; bootstrap keeps error-return fallback
native-runtime-init: initialize heap, symbol table, and std binding table
native-std-bindings: ucrt/kernel32 bridge planned
linking: static and dynamic modes are planned; dynamic path is currently exercised via host linkers
llvm/c/rust: optional accelerators only; portable bytecode remains canonical
cross-compilation: host-native
object-emission: scaffold stub only; not a fully linkable object writer yet
static-linking-path: planned via platform archiver + CRT selection
dynamic-linking-path: linker invocation wiring exists; output viability depends on a real object writer
link-skipped: no platform linker found in PATH
minimal-native-executable: bootstrap PE/ELF/Mach-O writer (functional exit stub; not full bytecode lowering)

evidence.claim_boundary: Native probe emits a minimal executable artifact; full Sanskript bytecode lowering to a native binary remains open.

milestones.title: Sanskript can target web without handwritten JavaScript application code

notes: HTML shell embeds canonical bytecode JSON; in-browser Sanskript VM runs application logic.
No handwritten JavaScript application handlers; host bridge is not required for app semantics.
tier plan_only for inventory; implementation_state functional for bytecode-in-html delivery.

milestones.title: The repo no longer requires Python/Rust for ordinary Sanskript development

evidence.claim_boundary: Development scope records a packaged bootstrap path, but repository tooling and verification still run through the host Python implementation.

milestones.title: Sanskript can express all current .sskyp examples in machine prose
Python/Rust remain only optional bootstrap, compatibility, or contributor convenience paths

evidence.policy: M20 requires M19 plus explicit optional-bootstrap documentation
evidence.claim_boundary: M19 is bootstrap-scoped only, so Python/Rust are not yet optional-only for the real repository workflow.

milestones.title: Sanskript standard library covers text, collections, files, JSON, CLI, HTTP, and tests
Sanskript can implement a useful CLI app without Python/Rust code
Sanskript can implement a useful web app without Python/Rust app code
Sanskript can implement a useful desktop/productivity app
Sanskript can implement a useful game loop and asset pipeline
Sanskript can implement research/data scripts
Sanskript can implement the VM core in rakṣita

evidence.claim_boundary: Subset corpus proves a Sanskript-authored VM-core sketch runs under the current host VM; it is not a retired native rakshita VM.

claim_boundary: Milestone ticks require automated proof in this report. Host-replay bootstrap (Phases 18–19) does not close M9–M15.

honesty_gates.policy: Phase 28 full independence requires every M0-M20 row to pass with claim_allowed=true. Bootstrap/subset/native-probe evidence is retained as useful progress but cannot close independence.
